meta.title: GwangMin Jeon | Service Planner Turning Data & AI into Decisions
meta.description: Portfolio of GwangMin Jeon, a service planner who builds information where none existed and connects it to real decisions.

hero.name: And a curious 'Human API' — GwangMin Jeon.
hero.title: Coding writer, writing engineer.
hero.tagline: I build information where none exists, and connect it to services that change how people decide and act.

profile.lead: Someone who finds joy in the process, not just the goal
profile.body: When problems come up, I stay calm and look for a way forward instead of panicking. I take deadlines seriously once we've agreed on them, and I'm never afraid to ask when I don't know something — learning is part of the job. Making a positive impact, on the team and beyond, is what motivates me most.

about.heading: About
about.intro: I've grown beyond simply collecting data into someone who connects data to information and services people can actually use. Over two years and seven months as a national R&D researcher, I built data from scratch in areas where none existed, analyzed it, and turned it into information used for real decisions. I later expanded from data analysis into service planning and generative AI through graduate school in AI and the Microsoft AI School program.

highlights.title: Building the evidence for decisions where no data existed
highlights.body: While working on a self-driving special transport vehicle project for people with disabilities, the pilot region had no related data at all. I ran field surveys to build baseline data, combined it with three years of usage data, and ran spatial analysis to visualize key mobility patterns — providing the evidence used to select pilot routes for autonomous driving. This taught me that the value of data isn't the analysis output itself, but building trustworthy information and connecting it to real decisions.

highlights.title: Planning services that reach users, not just analysis
highlights.body: I realized that even great data and insights fail to create value if users can't easily act on them. In a team project, I defined budget management difficulty while traveling as the core problem and led the concept for a travel expense-tracking service, 'NOTO,' designing the full flow from data collection to storage, analysis, and visualization. I learned that service planning means understanding what information users need and using data to deliver it most effectively.

highlights.title: Designing better decisions with AI
highlights.body: In the NOTO project, I used Azure Document Intelligence and GPT-4o Vision to automatically extract and structure receipt data, reducing manual input for users. In a school-zone child accident risk prediction project, I focused not just on predictive accuracy but on making results understandable and actionable through a Streamlit dashboard. I believe AI's role isn't the technology itself but making users' information discovery and decisions more efficient, and I continue to study advances in LLM technology.

about.closing: Building on my experience of creating information where none existed and connecting it to real decisions, I want to grow into a planner who turns data into services that change how people judge and act.

experience.org: Korea Association of Persons with Physical Disabilities (KAPPD) · Institute for Living and Environment of Persons with Disabilities
experience.summary: Led national R&D projects on transportation for people with disabilities, owning the full research process from data collection to analysis, visualization, and reporting.

projects.title: Autonomous Special Transport Vehicle for People with Disabilities
bullets: Collected and cleaned field survey data and built it into a database
Derived mobility patterns of transportation-disadvantaged users through QGIS-based spatial analysis and visualization
Selected candidate pilot routes for autonomous driving based on the analysis

projects.title: Standard Model for Regional Low-Floor Buses
bullets: Researched operating technology for the low-floor seated bus standard model
Planned educational content by collecting, classifying, and comparing domestic and international research and indicators
Planned and ran a competition related to low-floor buses
Analyzed legislation and drafted amendments to mandate related training

experience.footnote: Produced research outcomes including academic paper publications and reports for participating research projects

projects.title: AI-Based Travel Expense Tracker 'NOTO'
projects.subtitle: Microsoft AI School Team Project (2nd & Final) · Public launch planned H1 2027
bullets: Proposed "budget management difficulty while traveling" as the shared problem and led planning of the core service concept (expense tracking app)
Built a receipt OCR parsing and structuring module using Azure Document Intelligence and GPT-4o Vision
Implemented Azure Cosmos DB integration with full CRUD data operations
Built a spend-analysis engine producing category spend ratios, budget-overrun alerts, and daily trends
Built a FastAPI-based REST API server (6 endpoints) and deployed to Azure App Service / Static Web Apps
Designed and ran the full extract → transform → load → analyze data pipeline as a team project

projects.title: School-Zone Child Accident Risk Prediction Model
projects.subtitle: Microsoft AI School Team Project (1st)
bullets: Proposed the initial idea (an infrastructure-data-based child-friendly city diagnostic service); the team refined the direction into accident risk prediction
Built a prediction model combining safety facility and accident data from 142 school zones in Seongnam and Gwangmyeong with roughly 4,000 road-view images
Owned modeling and validation using Logistic Regression (L1/Lasso, SMOTE, OOF cross-validation)
Built a Streamlit/Folium dashboard to visualize results and a policy simulator to analyze the impact of adding facilities

projects.title: Autonomous Special Transport Vehicle for People with Disabilities

bullets: Collected and cleaned field survey data and built it into a database

Selected candidate pilot routes for autonomous driving based on the analysis
